name: Preston Board Gamers
short_description: We're a group of non traditional board gamers in Preston!
full_description: We're a Preston based gaming community of non-traditional board and card games. If you've never played anything except Monopoly before then never fear. We're a welcoming bunch, happy to teach any newcomers and help them enjoy the world of board gaming! We play a broad range of games, from social games like Blood On The Clock Tower, to worker placement games such as Dune Imperium. You don't need to bring anything along, as most peeps bring some games from their own collections, although more games is never a bad thing, and gives us more choice. We make sure everyone is included and has a game to play. We’ve been meeting since August 2013, and currently meet every Monday at Empire Services Club, Hartington road. We meet upstairs in the function room, which is to the left of the bar. Games start at 7:00pm with the aim of finishing around 11pm. It's free to attend Preston Board Gamers, but we ask that you buy a few drinks whilst playing games to support the venue.

name: Worcester Board Gamers (WoBoG)
short_description: Free weekly board games in a local pub
full_description: Come along and join us at The King's Head, Sidbury every Wednesday from 6:30ish for all things modern board gaming! We're a friendly, inclusive bunch, who are happy to teach you if you are new to the hobby, though feel free to bring any games along you would like to play if you already have a collection! We also do regular socials and 12 hour weekend board game events

name: All Mana of Games
frequency: Wednesdays 7pm - 10pm, Fridays 7pm - 11pm and 9am -8pm on the first Saturday of every month (from 2026)
short_description: Nuneaton, Warwickshire based tabletop club
full_description: Welcome to All Mana of Games , your local hub for all things tabletop! Based in the heart of Nuneaton, we bring people together to play, laugh, and roll dice, whether you're into board games, trading cards, role-playing, or wargaming. ​​All Mana of Games is more than just a tabletop gaming club, it’s a vibrant, inclusive community of players, storytellers, and adventurers! ​Our goal is to create a fun, safe, and welcoming space where people of all ages and experience levels can discover, enjoy, and connect through tabletop games. ​Whether you're rolling your first dice, slinging your favourite trading card deck, or planning your next RPG session, you’ll find your people here. ​ If you're looking to get involved, why not join our community Discord server ? ​

name: The Frome Board Games Club
location: The Bennett Centre (St John the Baptist Church Hall on Vicarage Street),Frome,BA11 1PU
short_description: Friendly and relaxed group of people who enjoy playing a variety of board, card and war games.
full_description: Long standing, friendly group that formed over 20 years ago now. We meet up in FROME every Thursday at 7pm. Our club has a relaxed atmosphere and a mix of people who enjoy playing a variety of board, card and war games old and new. We also have some that play X-Wing, Magic the Gathering and D&D. It all depends on what we fancy playing and what everyone has decided to bring. See our website for more details or perhaps let us know you're interested via our BGG page. See links.

name: Shuffle & Roll
short_description: Pop up board game night
full_description: Join us for our Monthly Board Game night. There are a wide selection of games on offer, with help on hand for rules & tips if needed! 6/6:30- 9pm. Relax and friendly atmosphere, See you there!

name: Portsmouth On Board
short_description: Weekly face to face gaming group
full_description: We are a gaming group that meet weekly in Portsmouth. We also have 3 all day events each year and a twice monthly day time meet. See our Facebook group and Meetup.com group for details of events https://m.facebook.com/groups/PortsmouthOnBoard/?ref=share https://www.meetup.com/portsmouth-on-board/ We are also on Instagram https://instagram.com/portsmouthonboard?igshid=OGQ5ZDc2ODk2ZA==

name: Oakham Board Game & Roleplaying Club
short_description: Our aim is to bring a range of tabletop & role-play gaming to the local community of Oakham, Rutland.
full_description: We are run as a not-for-profit club, with all proceeds going towards operating overheads, building of the clubs own library of games, subsidising of annual social activities and even charitable donations to a local mental health organisation 'Pepper's- A Safe Place'. Sessions run on Wednesday evenings between 1930 and 2230 at a cost of £3.50 per person, anyone over the age of 18 is welcome to join in. Our spacious and well equipment venue allows for a variety of games, across multiple tables, and such provisions on-site include a lounge area, fully equiped kitchen, toilet, air-conditioning, heating and ample natural light. Plenty of free parking is also available in the close vicinity. Please message us via Facebook or WhatsApp before hand to check that we have space available at the tables. Once you have attended your first session and deemed a member of the club, you will be invited to our private WhatsApp group for communication, games planning and general gaming chat! #letthegoodtimesroll If there is a particular game you are interested in playing, the chances are a fellow member will have it in their private collection (and I'm sure will be happy to bring it along for all to enjoy!) Alternatively you can view Oakham Board Game & Roleplaying Club's very own library of games (that are always kept on site!), and accessible for everyone to play and enjoy via the below link - https://boardgamegeek.com/collection/user/OakhamRPG

name: Snowdonia Dragons
short_description: We're a group of board gamers from North Wales who arrange various game groups and events which are open for anyone to join.
full_description: Our main club is currently held in Llanfairfechan, with members who join us from Conwy, Llandudno, Bangor, Anglesey, Caernarfon, Pwllheli and many other places. If you'd like to come along, you're welcome to join us for just one evening, every now and again or every week. We have 10-15 attendees most weeks, playing a huge variety of games. We're always will to teach new games and introduce newcomers to the hobby.

name: Pennsylvania Exeter Board Games
short_description: A weekly open-to-all board games group
full_description: A friendly place to play all types of board games with old friends and new! All welcome! You’re very welcome to bring games with you but it’s not required. £3.50 / session. 18+ only.

name: Faringdon Games Club
location: The Corn Exchange, Gloucester St, Faringdon, SN7 7JA,Faringdon,SN7 7JA
short_description: Join the Faringdon Games Club for epic adventures in D&D, MTG, Pokémon, board games, and more. Every Sunday evening we unite gamers for camaraderie, competition, and critical rolls! 🎲🌟
full_description: Faringdon Games Club is the ultimate gathering spot for game enthusiasts. We are a community of gamers, families, children and adults who dive into immersive D&D campaigns, challenge fellow Planeswalkers in Magic: The Gathering, trade and battle in Pokémon showdowns, and explore a diverse range modern board games. Meeting every fortnight on Sunday evenings, our club promotes camaraderie, friendly competition, and the thrill of the game. Whether you're a seasoned gamer or just starting out, there's an epic adventure waiting for you here! 🎲🌟

name: Three Spires Gaming
short_description: Local community wargamig group who help people with their mental wellbeing by the power of tabletop gaming
full_description: We are a non profitable community group and our aim is to help people with their mental health through the hobby of tabletop gaming. These can be war games like Warhammer 40K,Star wars Legion, MESBG or Skirmish games like Warcry, Moonstone. Even card games like Magic the Gathering and pokemon. Role play games have a huge positive influence on people's mental health. We offer people a safe and secure place to come and play these games with like minded people. I'm doing this we hope to evolve people's coping mechanisms as well as their skill set so that people can better manage their mental health. However we do also offer the chance for them to talk about their mental health, ask questions or seek advice.

full_description: A small group for local gamers to meet and play/socialise every Thursday

full_description: The East Cambridgeshire TTRPG club is an inclusive community of roleplaying game fans who meet weekly on Thursday evenings from 7pm in Victoria Hall, Fordham, Cambridgeshire. name: Aberdeen Social Board Games Group
short_description: A friendly, social board games group who play everything from Dobble to Splendour to Ticket to Ride to Scythe!
full_description: We are a friendly, welcoming group of board gamers who play a variety of types of board game, from social games like Love Letters and Exploding Kittens, traditional games like Scrabble and Cluedo, to Euro games, heavier board games and Ameritrash board games as well. We are a social group who enjoy meeting up to play games, chat and have a good time, and everyone is welcome to bring their own games to sessions: we play everyone's games, and the emphasis is on having a fun time in a relaxing environment!

If you’ve got a game you’d like to play, bring it along and you’re sure to find someone to play with. Alternatively, just bring yourself, friends, and family and join a game at our tables. We also do Ad-hoc additional events, playing both in-person and online. Please check us out on Facebook (worthing blood on the clocktowe). Our regular monthly event can also be found on Comics, Games and Coffee's website www.cgc-emporium.com

name: Sandbach Board Games
frequency: The first Saturday of the month (usually)
short_description: A family friendly group who play board games once a month.
full_description: A monthly board game meetup particularly aimed at families with young children who often struggle to attend this kind of event. We’re a friendly group of gamers (with and without kids) who meet up on the first Saturday of the month to play games. We enjoy modern board games like Pandemic, Ticket to Ride, and any recent Kickstarter deliveries. £5 per family per session. Tea, coffee and squash provided, feel free to bring your own snacks.

name: Nuneaton Model and Games Club
short_description: Come along, make new friends, then try to kill each other! 😂
full_description: We are a friendly group of people who enjoy playing many different games. 40K, Bolt Action, Black Seas, Barons War, What a Cowboy, Dead Man's Hand, Aeronautica and Magic the Gathering to name a few. Come along and join us. WE charge £4 entry to help cover the cost of hiring the room

name: St Albans Board Games Club
location: Scout Hut, End of Chene Drive on the Left, Off Waverley Road,St Albans,AL3 5QP
frequency: Alternate Mondays and Wednesdays at 7pm plus one Saturday a month
short_description: One evening a week and a monthly Saturday session in St Albans, Hertfordshire
full_description: A friendly all ages all games club that meets on a regular basis with alternate Monday and Wednesday sessions in St Albans. We also hold monthly all day events- regulars, newbies and family's are always welcome! We play all types of games, from Catan and Terraforming Mars through to the latest from kickstarter- someone's always got a new game they'd like to get to table. You can find out more about us on our facebook group and also on Board Game Arena! Subs You can pay for sessions (in preference order!): Bank transfer: Account: 24661997 Sort Code: 23-05-80 Cold hard cash (or we can do change normally!). PayPal (friends & Family): @stabgc The club is a place where like-minded individuals with a passion for strategy, tactics and gaming can come together to enjoy their hobby. The Monday events are held every two weeks 7pm – 11pm at the St Mark's Church Centre, Church Lane, Colney Heath, St Albans, Hertfordshire AL4 0NH. (It's the community hall next to the Church, you can park outside or on the road for free.) Wednesday events are held every two weeks 7pm – 11pm at the 3rd St Albans Scout Hut Oak Yard, Chene Drive, Waverley Road, St Albans, AL3 5QP. We also hold monthly game days at the same Scout Hut and these run from 1.30pm – 11pm and involve lots of gaming of all types of games. The next all day event can be found on the events listings. Mondays are £4, Wednesdays £4 and the all day Saturday events are £6. This covers the hire of the halls. We are more than happy to teach people games and help people through so please don't be worried about coming if you are new to board games. Feel free to bring your games or just come along and play what is there. We play anything from quick easy card games to 3 hour long epics and anything in between.

name: People's Meeples
short_description: Not-for-profit running board games events in Hartlepool
full_description: People's Meeples exists to promote the social and education benefit for gaming. We meet every Saturday night for board games with a £1 voluntary donation.

name: Playtest Portsmouth
location: Dice Board Game Lounge, 149 Albert Road,Portmsouth,PO4 0JW
frequency: The second Sunday of every month, 11am–5pm
short_description: A monthly meet up for games designers and playtesters on the South Coast
full_description: Playtest Portsmouth brings together board game designers and playtesters in and around Portsmouth (England). We hold regular face to face events where designers can share their prototypes and get valuable feedback from playtesters. All designers are invited to join us, first timers with a rough sketch for a game are just as welcome as regular designers with full prototypes that are ready for market. Playtesters of all levels of experience are welcome, we need you and your insights to help us shape how a game develops from a rough idea all the way to its final artwork. As well as getting designers & playtesters together our goal is to create a community of designers on the south coast who can support each other by sharing ideas, creativity, sharing materials for making prototypes and useful links to suppliers, printers and on-line advice.

name: Staffordshire Wargaming Guild CIC
location: Crown Wharf Crown Street Stone ST15 8QN,Stone Staffordshire,ST15 8QN
frequency: Every Tuesday & Thursday 6pm till late at Crown Wharf in Stone - Every Sunday 4.30 till late in Moddershall
short_description: Staffordshire Wargaming Guild is a Wargaming/Tabletop Charity community gaming group.
full_description: A Wargaming/Tabletop community gaming group that raises mental & social health awareness by connecting people of all ages and backgrounds through tabletop gaming in person and online. We support local charities by running events in our community. Get off your computer and consoles instead and try Face to Face Gaming!! Wargaming - Weekly games and tournaments - Bolt Action, K47, Blood Bowl, Warmachine, Warhammer 40k, AOS, and many more. TTRPGs - Huge variety of tabletop games - Dungeons and Dragons, Call of Cthulhu, Fallout RPG, Pathfinder 2e, Alien RPG, Terminator RPG, Dr. Who Adventures, The Expanse, Blades in the Dark, Animal Adventures, Delta Green, Wrath & Glory RPP, Konosuba TTRPG, Dread RPG etc. #notjustdnd Yearly charity events - 10+ hour events, tournaments, raffle, Dungeons and Dragons EPIC and unique RPGs Message our Facebook page for more info. www.facebook.com/staffswg Play more Games!! #rollformentalhealth

name: Mid Sussex Meeples
frequency: Monthly Game Days, Sundays, 10am thru 6pm, Dates Available on our Website.
short_description: Our group is based in Burgess Hill for beginners and more experienced players, from the young right through to the young at heart, to learn, teach and play these fabulous games.
full_description: We hold our game days at Cyprus Hall, Cyprus Road, Burgess Hill, RH15 8DX . Cyprus Road Car Park, at the rear of the hall hosts free parking on Sundays. Entrance is just £5 per person for the day, inclusive of Tea, Coffee, Squash, Biscuits and the occasional slice of cake. Your welcome to come and go throughout the day, pop out for some lunch or bring some back to carry on gaming. We have no age restriction and welcome any well behaved children in with an accompanied adult right through to those young at heart pensioners looking for some extra activities to partake in and of course every one in between. We are on the level with disabled parking at the door and have fully accessible W/C facilities. It is truly a hobby that brings everyone together and a fantastic sight seeing multiple generations sat down interacting over some card, paper and plastic. All money taken is put back into the club, securing the venue for further events, to promote the club within the local area and to add games to the ever growing library available for you to use at the events. Our library, currently, consists of 120+ titles , many of which are brought to our events for people to play. Other members also bring their own games with them to also play, meaning that your always spoilt for choice. We will also have a ' For Sale ' table in case any gamers are looking to raise funds for new games. So if you are looking to lighten your shelves then reluctantly bring down your unwanted board games and put them on the table, together with a post it note with your name and asking price. Buyers can then find you at the group. Recent studies have shown that Board Gaming in general can help numerous social and anxiety issues as well as keeping your brain fully fit and active. As someone who does suffer with depression and anxiety, the world of board gaming has opened up so much to me. The community as a whole is one I feel grateful to be a part of both locally with our associated clubs in the area and on a larger scale, attending conventions. So we ask you to come and experience it for yourselves with ourselves or any of our associated groups in the area. We hope you can make it and look forward to seeing you.

name: Bridgend Board Gamers
short_description: Come and join our small friendly group to meet your cardboard cravings. Currently running weekly sessions in Bridgend.
full_description: Bridgend Board Gamers & Firestorm Games present MONDAY and TUESDAY BOARD GAMES Come and join a lovely bunch of gamers in our weekly game night every Monday and Tuesday at Firestorm Games in Bridgend town centre from 6pm onwards. Everyone is welcome to join us as we play all kinds of tabletop games to suit all levels of experience. This includes party, card & gateway games to more complex and longer Euro Games and Ameritrash (https://tabletopbellhop.com/gaming-advice/euro-vs-ameritrash/). Every game will start with a teach so, if any players are unfamiliar with it, you can be sure you will not be alone. We are sure to have a game that suits you and please bring your own to help us to discover our new favourite game. THE VENUE We have recently partnered with Firestorm Games as we share their enthusiasm for gaming and philosophy that playing is important for us all to keep our heads above water and keep keepin’ on. We have tables reserved in their Arena gaming area which is a private gaming room with massive tables perfect for stretching out our cardboard on. We have agreed a £3 per head table charge and are encouraged to book tickets via their Battlefields Event page here: https://www.thebattlefields.co.uk/index.php?seo=events-at-the-battlefields&venue=5&category=0&resultpage=2 Although Firestorm Bridgend does not have a hot food and drink facility, they are happy for us to eat and drink there so we have partnered up with Corvo Lounge so for those who would like to order from here we will arrange a group pick up on the evening. https://order.thelounges.co.uk/corvo/takeaway/menus/takeaway-food There are a few places to park nearby - you can park in the town centre itself after 6pm once the barriers come down. Many use the Brackla 3 Street car park (behind the old Wilkos Store), CF31 1BZ. It's free after 6pm and charges are only 50p for 1/2 hour before that (£2 for up to 3 hours) and there is also The Rhiw car park which I believe is still free for the first 3 hours. We have a WhatsApp group chat if you are interested in joining, just message me direct. Please click the 'Going' and 'Interested' buttons if you can as it's good to know how many tables to book and I am keen to get the word out to grow our group. Message us direct if you need to find out more. Hope to see you there!

name: Clubhouse Gaming
short_description: Bringing people together through their love of games and building an inclusive community for everyone that wants to be a part of it.
full_description: WELCOME! The new home of Newcastle board games, RPGs and tabletop hobbies, Clubhouse welcomes those that have nowhere to play. Sick of church halls and gaming on pasting tables once a month? Clubhouse specialises in regularly providing a safe, inclusive, environment for everyone. Not only do we put on regular socials, we also do big, full day, specialised events. Clubhouse knows games are best played with likeminded friends. We're creating a community of people that want to learn how to play games and find others. ​Games are better when shared with friends - old and new. SOCIAL GAMING Games are always better played with other people. Like so many others when we started this hobby, there was no one to play with and nowhere to play it. Clubhouse aims to change that. We want to bring people together to create a community that unites players from everywhere. REGULAR NIGHTS We run weekly, focused nights - targeting different parts of the community. A regular RPG night, tabletop game night, or just board games, there's something for everyone. Newcastle gaming has never been so good. COMMUNITY Talk to us about the games you love, the games you want to play. Clubhouse is here to talk about all aspects of the hobby. This is your space to talk about the things that mean the most to you. Your passion is our passion. Everyone has a voice in Clubhouse.

name: Exmouth Boardgames Group
short_description: Friendly group of all ages and backgrounds
full_description: Friendly group of all ages and backgrounds, though as we usually meet in a pub it is 18+ unless accompanied by an adult A welcoming group, of beginners and hard-core players alike, from connect 4 to twilight imperium, we will play it all! We would love new members and everyone is welcome! Gaming is completely Free, though we ask you buy a drink from the barfolk who kindly let us play there. We play in The Park Hotel Pub every Thursday, opposite the amazing Level1 games store. We also meet in Wings bar on a Tuesday 6.30pm onwards Bring your own games, or come play ours, between our regulars we have 1,000s!

name: BOARD FOR GAMING
short_description: Fortnightly boardgames club in Cranbrook
full_description: We meet at Rockbeare Village Hall on the 1st and 3rd Thursday of each month (6.30pm-9.30pm), as well as occasional Sundays. Cost is £1 per session (50p for unwaged). We are a friendly and inclusive group who play a wide variety of modern boardgames.

name: Waterside Boardgaming
location: St. Andrew's Church, Beaulieu Road, Dibden Purlieu,Southampton,SO45 4PT
short_description: Boardgamers of the Hythe and Waterside area in the New Forest
full_description: Started early 2022 this club has come a long way in a short amount of time, offering board gamers of the new forest, Southampton and often people as far away as Poole, Winchester & Portsmouth quality and interesting events. Our vision is to create a welcoming, community-led space where people of all ages and backgrounds can connect through the joy of board gaming. Monthly Sunday events, various dates, 14:00-19:00 - Great bus routes, ample parking, wheelchair friendly - £3pp Space - A large, accessible church hall with plenty of room for games - big or small. Great parking and bus routes. Excellent facilities. Hosting - We operate a hosting service at our events to match attendees to the right games and groups, along with game recommendations. Library - Our curated games library is free to use at events. We have a range of great games suitable for all ages and preferences. Teaching - We operate a welcome table where we teach quick and easy games as well as having regular teach tables run by volunteers. Raffles - The game raffles at our events are real head-turners! Thrills and smiles all-round. A Range of brilliant prizes to be had. Tuck Shop - Our affordable range of tea, coffee, drinks and snacks will give you the energy you need to master your opponents. Delicious! Accessories - We keep a range of useful bits and bobs to keep games rolling from players wanted signs, to dice and trays, to scoresheets. Bring and Buy - They say one man’s trash is another man’s treasure... We operate a bring and buy table where you can buy or sell games on the day. We strive to make our events inclusive, affordable, and accessible—whether at regular meet-ups, online sessions, tournaments, marathons or charity fundraisers. By offering friendly hosting, welcome tables, and opportunities for newcomers to learn, we provide a supportive environment that combats loneliness and encourages lasting friendships. Together, we celebrate the hobby, strengthen ties with local clubs and businesses, and give back through charitable work benefiting both our community and beyond. Join us.

name: Thame Games Club
short_description: A tabletop gaming club, free to join, and free to come along!
full_description: If you enjoy board games, and are over 18, then why not come along and join us for a Tabletop games evening in Thame Snooker Club’s function room. It is free to attend and also to join the club. Simply come and go as you please. Enjoy an evening playing your favourite games or learning new ones. You can bring your own games and buddy up with other players or choose from a selection of member owned games. It is an easy walk from most places in Thame and at the back of the High Street or driving just off Wellington Street also has lots of free parking in the evening. The function room venue offers plenty of space and a range seating and tables are available to allow many more games to hit the table. The club has great facilities, toilets in the room and a shared bar with the Snooker Club. We have plenty of space but it is always great to get an idea of numbers. Book now and we look forward to meeting you on the evening.

name: Gloucester Boardgame Group

short_description: Meeting fortnightly for tabletop games in Dorset.

location: Elmscroft Community Centre, Coronation Grove GL2 0SS,Gloucester,Gl2 0SS

name: Crookes & Crosspool Tabletop Gaming
short_description: Tabletop gaming club is Crookes, Sheffield
full_description: Established in the summer of 2021, we're a tabletop gaming group, located at the award-winning Crookes Social Club in the (you guessed it) Crookes area of Sheffield (close to Crosspool, Walkley, Broomhill and the city centre). The club is open for gaming on Thursday evenings, 6pm - 11pm, where we can be found in the lounge. Nearby parking is free and plentiful and, for those of you taking the bus, the club is a 5-minute walk from a 52/52a bus stop. We're open to all ages and tabletop interests, whether it's a wargame, board game or card game - all are very much welcome! Attendees of the club play a vast and varied number of games, whether it's Sci-fi, fantasy, historical, and even quirky and much-loved card and board games! So, please, come along, buy a drink - and get ready to roll some dice and/or shuffle some cards! PLEASE NOTE - it's free entry, though all punters are asked to buy at least one drink from the bar (after all, it's free entry!)

name: Redditch Tabletop Gamers

We aim to be open and friendly, so if you want to come along to see what the hobby is all about, want to play new games that you've never tried before, or even if you just need a room to play a game that you already know, then we look forward to having you join us. We meet at Christ Church, Chineham from 1800 - 2300 on alternating Saturdays. See our socials for specific dates

name: Cardboard Emperors
location: The White Bear, 138 Kennington Park Rd,London,SE11 4DJ
short_description: London-based board game club focusing on historical and war and strategy games.
full_description: We meet every Monday evening from 5pm at the White Bear pub in Kennington, just opposite the tube station. We tend to play historical and war and strategy games, such as Twilight Struggle, the COIN system games, hex and counter wargames, and similar. However we also play Ameritrash and Euro-style games, and often end the night with a trick-taking game. We are a very warm and welcoming bunch. Most Mondays we get between 10-15 attendees. We organise our games using our BGG guild (https://boardgamegeek.com/guild/3663), and also have a Discord server and Facebook page. Contact James for more details.

name: Wyvern Tabletop Club
frequency: Each month on the second Sunday from 6pm
short_description: Mid Worcestershire's preeminent based-in-a-village-hall games night, attracting gamers from across the county, for modern board games, RPGs, miniatures, and card games.
full_description: When? second Sunday of each month, 6pm - 9:30pm Where? Bishampton Villages Hall, WR10 2LY In the heart of Wychavon, Mid Worcestershire, we've created the kind of board games and tabletop club that we always wished we had. Wyvern Tabletop Games Club is like a pop-up board game cafe... but with a limited choice of refreshments! It's like a board games night at a pub... if everybody in the pub was a gamer, and the pub is actually a modern village hall! And you can bring your own drinks. More on our website: https://sites.google.com/view/wyvern-tabletop/ Open to all, any background, orientation or identity. We value and promote diversity within the tabletop gaming community. And whether you're an experienced gamer, or completely new to the hobby, or anywhere in between, there's a place for you at the table. You do not have to be local or have any connection to the village.

name: Winchcombe Board Game Group

name: Artefact - Stirchley Roleplaying Society
location: The British Oak, Penn Room, 1364, Pershore Road, Stirchley, Birmingham, B30 2XS,Birmingham,B30 2XS
short_description: Roleplaying group playing a diverse range of games
full_description: We are a close knit group arranging roleplaying sessions held online and in-person at The British Oak, Penn Room, 1364, Pershore Road, Stirchley, Birmingham, B30 2XS. We host online games twice a week, and a in-person session in The British Oak in Stirchley every 2nd and 4th Wednesday each month. Come along to join us for various ongoing games and sessions. There are long campaigns, or short scenarios from a range of different game settings and types. We are currently playing Vaesen, Dungeons & Dragons 5e, Blades in the Dark, and Masks. Previous games have included Kult, Alien, Dread, Paranoia, Warhammer Fantasy Roleplay, City of Mist, Troika and Numenera. Most group organisation is conducted via our facebook group, so please contact us there if you are interested in joining for any of our games. All members agree to adhere to our Code of Conduct, detailed in the files section. Please familiarise yourselves with it and abide by it.

name: Preston's Gamer's Guild

name: STAG

We also occasionally host free all day Saturdays. We play a wide range of modern board and card games, usually medium weight, often closing on a large group social deduction game. Please join the Facebook group to let us know you're coming. We are Guild 1820 on BoardGameGeek and post session reports there.

name: Wickford Board Game Club
location: The Darby Digger, Radwinter Avenue, Wickford, SS12 9PT,Wickford,SS120JA
short_description: Friendly gaming - everyone is welcome!
full_description: Welcome to Wickford Board Game Club! We are proud to represent Wickfordians from all walks of life who come together every Friday at the Darby Digger to share their passion for board games. Whether you've never played a game before, have worn out your family copies of Monopoly and Risk, are venturing into the hobby with Ticket To Ride or Pandemic, or know your Twilight Imperium from your Twilight Struggle, we are open to all. Between our membership we have hundreds of titles but are always looking for new things to play. Membership is totally free and there is no charge to attend our events either! Please check out our rules we abide by to keep the group a happy and welcoming place for all. We are a gaming club for adults only (18+)

name: Warrington Board Games Club
short_description: We are a casual gaming group that run a weekly games night playing a wide variety of games.
full_description: We are a casual gaming group which means we do not require members to turn up every week to keep their place in the club. We meet every Wednesday evening (7PM to 11PM) in the function room of the King's Head in Warrington, to play board games, chat and socialise. The function room is located at the rear of the pub and accessed by following the corridor to the side of the bar. As modern board gamers we're often asked 'what type of games do we play' - you won't find monopoly, scrabble etc being played as these are “traditional” boardgames. We play “hobby” boardgames, a good starting point would be to check out TableTop on YouTube. It's an online TV show that gives a great overview of the types of games we play at the club. We use the MEETUP website & app to arrange our games each week (see link further down), take a look at the comments section for an upcoming Wednesday, and you'll see the games available to play. If you see something you fancy, comment on it to let the game host know you want join the game. Alternatively you can turn up on the night to see what games are on offer, but availability will vary and sometimes there may not be any open spaces left. You are also more than welcome to bring your own games along and post them up on the comments section specifying how many open spots you have for people to join as this helps members to track when a game has become full. If you're still unsure about what to play, message an organiser and we'll point you in the right direction, introduce you to other members, and answer any questions you may have. Our rules are simple: To cover the use of the function room everyone must buy a drink from the bar, be responsible, respectful, and most importantly, have fun! Member fees to cover Meetup costs are £5 every six months, and are collected around April and September. We hope to see you soon!

name: Sheffield Nerd Collective
short_description: Discord based nerdy social group !
full_description: The Sheffield Nerd Collective does various meetups in and around Sheffield including Board gaming at Patriot Games on Thursday nights from 6.30-10.30 in the main hall. The event is free. We always play a variety of complexities and different length games (fine for latecomers or leaving early) and all the games are listed for signing up to 4 days before the event so you'll have a seat reserved and know what you're playing in advance. We also meetup for a drink and burger beforehand! We're an 18+ group. feel free to join the discord or come say hi at a session !

name: Herefordshire Board Gamers
frequency: First Thursday / Third Sunday In person and other events too
short_description: Gaming, Fundraising, and knowledge sharing community
full_description: Free friendly inclusive events in person and online. Run a wide range of events and love supporting the community. Free community lending library of over 600 games. Knowledge base on how to run accessible and inclusive events Over £30,000 raised for causes since 2017

name: Verwood Board Game Club
short_description: A friendly club in Verwood, enjoying non-traditional board and card games
full_description: Verwood Board Games Group aims to promote friendship, fun and laughter through the medium of contemporary board games. We meet on the 2nd Wednesday of the month at The Swans pub (by Morrisons), 7pm to ~10pm . The group is open to adults only and free to join. We alternate between themed evenings and “Play it again” nights so that we get to play a variety of games as well as revisiting new favourites or games that caught our interest on another table. If you have a game that suits feel free to bring it with you, or come and try out something new from the selection brought along by the group hosts and regulars. Whether you are an experienced gamer, simply curious or wishing to make new friends, you can be guaranteed an enjoyable evening. Our host venue is a quiet and friendly pub with decent lighting and good food. It is situated in the Morrisons car park so there is plenty of free parking - go straight ahead when you enter the car park and pass the fuel station, then take a right just before the store entrance, you'll see the pub ahead of you. Please check our Facebook group for event dates/themes or to get in touch. We hope to see you at games night soon!

name: Medway Area Boardgamers
short_description: Maidstone-based boardgames group for North Kent and the Medway area.
full_description: We're a group in north Kent , who like to get together on a weekly basis and play board and card games. We're open to playing pretty much anything, but we typically play Eurogames and also modern examples of the "Thematic Games" style. We meet in the function room of a very friendly pub in Maidstone (The Style and Winch) every Wednesday. NOTE: We charge a £2 table fee per person to cover the room hire. NOTE: Since it's a pub, under 18s may come along, join but must be accompanied by an adult.

name: Ashford Board Games Club
location: The Rose Inn, Faversham Road,Ashford, Kent,TN24 9AJ
short_description: Board games of every type, every Sunday evening in Ashford, Kent.
full_description: Meet nearby boardgame players! Come to a session of the Board Games Club to play card, tile and board games of every kind: Carcassonne, Pandemic, Dominion, Small World, Galaxy Trucker, Agricola, Hare & Tortoise, Zooloretto, Settlers of Catan, Go or anything else. We meet every Sunday evening from 7pm to 11pm at The Rose Inn, Faversham Road, Ashford TN24 9AJ. If you are interested please join the Facebook Group: to keep up to date on dates and venues. Games players of every kind welcome. Just turn up and join in the games. Please bring any games you are interested in playing, or request them in advance.

name: Board In Mildenhall
location: St. John's Close, Mildenhall, Bury Saint Edmunds IP28 7NX,Mildenhall,IP28 7NX
frequency: Several times a week, depending on groups and events. Check our Discord for current plans.
short_description: Community events, free entry, open to all and for all levels of experence. Visit us at Mildenhalls local St.Johns Community Center.
full_description: We have wide variety of many games, ranging from group games like D&D all the way to solo games. Tailor groups to meet any level of player with casual basic games like Cluedo and King of Tokyo all the way up to the more complex meaty games like On Mars and Frost Punk. So no matter if looking for RpGs, and Warhammer matches, or Roll and Write games to discover. Groups play here daily and we aim to find the perfect game, and group for any interested. Contact via email or Discord to see what we have and see the times that best suit you.

name: Bromley Board Gamers
location: Petts Wood Methodist Church Halls BR5 1EB,Petts Wood,BR5 1EB
short_description: Friendly board games group based in the south east London borough of Bromley. Meets at Petts Wood Methodist church halls BR5 1EB every Monday evening 6-10 pm.
full_description: We are a group that has been meeting every Monday since 2018 , we play a wide variety of board games from heavy titles that take the evening to light fare where a table gets through many different games in a night ! Size wise we normally have 3-4 tables playing at each meeting. Our members range from Students to Retirees brought together by a shared love of board gaming and its community. We are a welcoming lot so if you are new to the hobby that's not a problem we'll ease you in. One thing that often stops people joining a new club is the fear of the unknown, unknown people, unknown location and not knowing what's going to happen. The rest of this description is a guide to how to signup for a monday event and what to expect. People signup on the facebook group to an event which is sent out to all member generally on a friday, They click "I'm going" in the facebook event to say they are attending. There is a small cover charge, this covers our hall rental and lets us know you are attending so we know roughly how many tables needed. There is a thread in that facebook event where people are talking about games they are bringing or want to play. People may chime in and some slots in these games are pre-organised . This is not mandatory and most games are sorted out on arrival. Turn up ! We are in the Parlour room of the Petts Wood methodist church. Come in the door at the rear of the church halls and turn left. Around 6 p.m. we will be setting up the tables, covers and chairs needed. People tend to bring bags of their games which are put on a communal table for usage, so there is always a good choice and you don't "have" to bring anything. At this point we introduce everyone and organise the people we have to tables as best we can. If you are new to the hobby we will introduce you with something lighter, if you see something you like you can join that table. All the tables will have a leader who will do the "teach" of the game so you don't have to know anything turning up. As games wrap up new ones are started, taught and played so most tables get through a few games in the evening. At 10p.m. we wrap up , tidy away the tables and chairs and head home. Some people leave earlier for trains etc. If you plan to leave early just let people know so we can plan shorter games to finish in time or where your absence doesn't interrupt the enjoyment of the other players. That's the rough outline of us and our the evenings, hope to see you along some evening.

name: Ribble Valley Gamers Guild
short_description: We're a Longridge based gaming community of non-traditional board and card games.
full_description: We're a friendly (yet mildly competitive 😜) group who love to play all things board game and RPG! We meet up every month upstairs at the Conservative Club, Berry Lane, Longridge and it's £5 entry. Everyone playing must be 18+. We're always looking to get games that we couldn't normally play to the table so there's always a mix of every type of board game! You have a game you wanna play but can't get it table? Bring it! You just wanna try a bunch of games come along and give them a try.

name: Neutral Ground
frequency: Mondays through Fridays 2pm til 10pm and Saturdays through Sundays 10am til 10pm
short_description: A place where all can come and play games. From casual groups, to more tournament focused events, there is a table for everyone.
full_description: We currently host the Monday Night Board Games group, Plymouth TrollSlayers, Exploding 8s, Baron Administrator Tournaments, Friday Fite Nite plus Pokemon, Yugioh, MTG, DND, Horus Heresy and anything else people want to play

name: Griffin Gaming Club Night
short_description: A casual gaming club playing any game system.
full_description: We set this club up to support our local play base, playing any game system, you will not find any gate keeping here. You are more than welcome to just hand put and chat if you don't fancy a game. The club is completely free! All you need to do is just turn up or you can arrange a game via our discord.

name: Ruislip BGGs
frequency: Monday evenings, for an 8 o'clock start
short_description: Board games enthusiasts who meet Monday evenings
full_description: We're a group of gamers who get together on Monday evenings to play board games - mostly medium/heavy Euro-style. We're fans of Power Grid, for example, while more recent favourites include Ark Nova and Obsession. However, we can be found playing Azul, Love Letter, Secret Hitler, Splendor and even Loopin' Louie… Our venue is just up the road from West Ruislip station (Central Line and Chiltern Railways), has a bus stop right outside and parking is available. We gather in time to get beers (other drinks are available) and start a game by 8 o'clock most Mondays. We have a WhatsApp group that we use for discussions, but there's always room for anyone turning up on the night. We also have a Guild on BGG: https://boardgamegeek.com/guild/4247

name: Swiggers
short_description: Wednesday evenings in central London
full_description: We meet on Wednesday evenings to play board games in the upstairs room at the Shipwright's Arms (88 Tooley Street, London SE1). This is next to London Bridge station (right by an entrance to the mainline station). People generally arrive between 6 and 6:30 pm. These days we've usually arranged who's playing what beforehand (on our WhatsApp group), but visitors are welcome to join in. The pub has a decent menu: food can be ordered (from the main bar, on the ground floor) until 8 pm and drinks are served until much later. We usually pack up between 10:30 and 11 pm, but this varies according to people's commitments. We welcome visitors and new members. If you have an interest in board games, get in touch and come along one Wednesday evening.

name: Highland Tabletop
location: Various locations in Fort William and the beautiful west coast.,Fort William,Ph336Wh
frequency: Once a month for more organised themed events, once a week for casual gaming.
short_description: Highland Adventurers Rolling the Dice
full_description: A board game and tabletop role playing game meetup at various exciting locations around the west Highlands.

name: Cambridge board game nights with Morat Games
short_description: A group by Morat Games for Cambridge board gaming enthusiasts. We run regular board game nights roughly every two weeks where you can play the latest games with other board game fans.
full_description: Fancy an evening of board gaming in Cambridge? We have large tables for gaming, a board games library, and plenty of people with a passion for board games. There are plenty of games on offer provided by our members, but if you have a great collection then please consider bringing a few games along. If you want to arrange to play something specific, feel free to use the comments or our Discord server to get a group together to play a particular game. We have to pay for the venue, so please be aware that we'll ask for £5 to cover costs (we take cards). Free parking is available at the centre, and we're only a short distance from the Cambridge North rail station. The night starts at 6pm and the doors will remain open until 6.30pm. If you're going to arrive later, then please join the Discord below so you can message me and I can let you in (there's no bell). We start to pack up at 10.30pm for 11pm lockup. I look forward to seeing you there! Please consider joining our Discord server to keep in touch about future events, chat about games, arrange games to play, and for late arrivals as above.

name: Kenilworth Board Games
short_description: Group for Kenilworth and surrounding areas to play and discuss board games. We play a range of light to heavy games. All welcome! :)
full_description: Group for Kenilworth and surrounding areas to play and discuss board games. We play a range of light to heavy games. All welcome! :) We bring a selection of modern tabletop board games to choose from. We rotate them each week, so there will always be variety. Please feel free to bring along games of your own. To make it easiest for you to jump into a game straight away, it's best to arrive at the start of the session and stay on, plus it's worth trying new games as well as ones we already know how to play. Location and parking: Parking is available at the venue. Public transport is easily accessible with stops for both the x17 and the 11 near by, in the centre of town. Train station is 10 minutes walk. The club costs £3 to attend, your first night is free. New members and guests are always welcome!

name: Grimsby Wargames Society
frequency: Every Thursday from 7.15pm, other days by arrangement
short_description: Board Games, Historical War Games, Warhammer & CCGs and more!
full_description: Grimsby Wargames Society (GWS) celebrated its 50th Anniversary this year and in that time the club has evolved into so much more. Originally GWS was specifically for Historical War Games but has evolved into so much more now. The two story building houses many large tables, and expertly created scenery for War Games and Warhammer. Board Game nights are thriving, with anywhere from 3-15 players on a Thursday night and games range from 5 minute fillers all the way to all day epics! We are always happy to accept new members or casual visitors. Contact us Facebook for more info!

name: M:TG London @ The Games Club
short_description: The Games Club was founded on the idea of providing a welcoming, relaxed environment for us to enjoy each other's company while playing an assortment of games.
full_description: Our primary focus is Magic: The Gathering, where we offer a tournament experience without the stresses of of things getting hyper-competitive. Whether you are a new player or a grizzled veteran, we have the right answer for you. We take all the fun of a home games night, and expand on it by providing a whole mess of elements to take things to the next level. More players, more games and even prizes for the winners! With a host of players young and old playing trading card games and board games, it is an oasis of fun in the heart of London. There's no membership fee or velvet rope here - come on down and join us for a great evening of gaming.

name: Hobby Workshop Gaming Club
short_description: Friendly gaming club with free tables, battle mats and terrain.
full_description: We have 9 tables in our dedicated gaming room available to book for free using our online booking system. There are enough battle mats and terrain for every table available to use for free. There's plenty of parking right outside the shop. We have a growing community of people playing many different games, why not join the Discord to get involved. If you feel a bit peckish or fancy freshly ground coffee, we also have a well stocked snack bar. We’ll also be running events so stay tuned to see what’s coming up and news on future expansions.

name: Bawdrip Board Game Club
short_description: Fornightly board game club in bridgwater, 5mins from M5
full_description: A new board game club just started up. Play games you know and love or learn a new game Twice a month on weds 7.30-9.30pm at Bawdrip Parish Hall. (usually 1st and 3rd weds of month) Non villagers very welcome A selection of games are provided or you can bring your own! Everyone is welcome, both casual gamers and serious gamers, family's groups or individuals. There is always a friendly game going you can join in. £1 towards room hire, feel free to bring drinks and snacks with you.

name: Board Together
location: Old Colliery Offices, Colliery Rd, Kiveton Park, Sheffield S26 6LR,Kiveton Park,S26 6LR
frequency: Once a month, the 3rd Friday of the month, 6:30pm
short_description: Board game group for people in the Kiveton and Wales area of Rotherham.
full_description: Do you know your "Clank" from your "Catan", your "Scythe" from your "Splendor", or your "Ark Nova" from your "Alchemists"... Well, if it's a yes (or if it's a no and your totally confused but intrigued by it all), then bring a board game if you fancy, or join in with a game that someone else has brought with them. It's on the third Friday of the month at Kiveton Park Community Development Trusts' building (the old pit colliery offices). Doors open at 6:30pm until 10:30pm. Take a look at the Board Together in Kiveton and Wales group for more information. Cost: £2.00 for adults, £1.00 for children for the whole 4 hours, or £1.00 for adults and 50p for children for 2 hours. All monies go back in to the running of the evening. Feel free to bring your own drinks and snacks.

name: Pudsey and Farsley Boardgame Club
short_description: Weekly boardgaming group meetup
full_description: We are a group that meets weekly on a Friday night, hosted at Leodis Games. Members bring games both new and old, with a huge variety of styles of games. We have a Facebook group which is used to organise specific games if required. Leodis games itself is a super clean and friendly space, offers good drinks and snacks too.

name: The Friends of General Haig
short_description: A friendly club, based in Worcestershire, mainly playing tabletop miniature wargames.
full_description: We are a friendly club meeting every fortnight on a Saturday afternoon to play tabletop miniature wargames. Always happy to welcome new gamers of any level of experience. (Children need to be accompanied.) You can see more about us on our web site here: https://fogh.blog

name: Maidstone Wargames Society
short_description: Local club with a 50-year history
full_description: We are a small, friendly, society covering a range of periods; Ancients through Medieval, Napoleonic, ACW, 20th Century; on land, at sea, or in the air. We also regularly venture into deep space or to worlds of fantasy. We are a pretty cosmopolitan bunch and anyone interested in other periods would be most welcome. There is always at least one game open to any member at each meeting.

name: The Role Play Haven - Archway Branch
short_description: RPH is the largest tabletop role-playing clubs in the UK. At all our branches, we offer a wide range of games such as D&D 5e, Cthulhu, Tales from the Loop, Traveller, Pathfinder, Alien and more!
full_description: RPH is the largest tabletop role-playing clubs in the UK. At all our branches, we offer a wide range of games such as D&D 5e, Cthulhu, Tales from the Loop, Traveller, Delta Green, Pathfinder, Alien, CyberPunk, Blades in the Dark, Star Trek, Runequest, and many more. A volunteer committee manages our branches that any member can stand for, and our profits are donated to a local charity voted in by our members. We believe in fun, safe, and charity gaming! Please see our website for more details.
