name: Boardgames in Truro
location: The Old Ale House, 7 Quay St, Truro, Cornwall, TR1 2HD,Truro,TR1 2HD
short_description: A bi-weekly games night in Truro, Cornwall.
full_description: Welcome to Boardgames in Truro, a bi-weekly casual boardgame night! It’s every other Monday from 6pm upstairs at the Old Ale House in Truro, Cornwall. Everyone is welcome, whether you’re an experienced gamer or completely new to boardgames. We play every type of game from classic boardgames, party games, and strategy games. Bring your own games to play or play other peoples. It is a fun and friendly environment to play your favourite games, discover new boardgames, or just enjoy a drink over a casual game.

name: Cornwall Boardgame Playtest Group
short_description: A playtest group meeting twice monthly in Falmouth for designers and boardgamers in Cornwall.
full_description: Welcome to Cornwall Boardgame Playtest Group! A twice-monthly playtest group currently meeting every other Sunday 2-4pm at the Moth and the Moon in Falmouth, Cornwall. Anybody and everybody is welcome! You don’t even need to be a designer or bring a game. You just need a positive attitude and willingness to help make games better. Players: relax, play some games and give feedback. Some games may be in-progress, unbalances and a bit wonky, but your help in making them better is half the fun! Designers: Bring games that are in any stage of development. They don’t need to be super fancy, and it’s in fact better if they are quite rough, as it makes it easier to change. Longer games may not get player to completion and make sure you also test other designer’s games.

name: Wargames Assocation of Reading
short_description: Wargames club in Reading playing all sorts of games!
full_description: The club was founded over 40 years ago and has since grown into one of the largest UK clubs. We regularly meet and the game systems played vary from early historical, sci fi and fantasy through to wild west and the year 40,000

name: Tabletop Manchester
short_description: Board game group meeting in central Manchester
full_description: As one of (if not the) largest and longest running board game groups in central Manchester, we have met once a week for 10 years (minus the pandemic) at The Wharf pub in Castlefield. Around 50 people turn up each week to play board games and card games of all kinds. With free parking and no charge for the event we run a casual “turn up and play” club full of friendly gamers of all levels of experience and tastes.

name: Phoenix Gaming Club
location: 5 Brooke Close, Rushden NN10 9YE... Just behind Orbit Tyres,Rushden,NN10 9YE
short_description: Phoenix Gaming Club meets every Tuesday evening from 6.45 - late at the Rushden Scout HQ, 5 Brooke Close, Rushden NN10 9YE...  Just behind Orbit Tyres.
full_description: Miniature games, Board and card games, and a regular RPG group means a variety of systems are played each week. We were also the first club in the WORLD to host tournaments for both Bolt Action & Burrows and Badgers. We also have yearly Kings of War events, and attend several events / festivals where we run public participation games. Please check out our Facebook page, or pop along on a club night, where your first nights gaming is FREE…

name: Wayfarer's League
short_description: Wayfarer’s League was born out of our passion for tabletop gaming, food and drink, and creating amazing moments with friends, old and new. Where we play. Where we connect.
full_description: Wayfarer's League is an online and in person community. We offer a variety of online table top role playing games through our Website and Discord server. Games are free to join. Our in person board game nights are every week in Godalming, at Wayfarer's Rest and is £8 a session and can be booked through our website. Please do not hesitate to be in touch via email if you have any questions, we would love to hear from you!

name: Around the Table
frequency: Every 2nd and 4th Friday evening of the month (start time will either be 7pm or 7:45pm - check Facebook page for further details).
short_description: A friendly twice-monthly games group, welcoming all-ages and levels of experience, as well as lots of cake!
full_description: Around The Table is a friendly games group welcoming people of all ages and levels of experience, so whether you're a lifelong gamer or a complete beginner you'll find a warm welcome awaiting you and experienced games teachers to help you find your way. We also have cake! We meet at Cirencester Baptist Church on the 2nd and 4th Friday of every month. All are welcome, but we do ask that any under-18s are accompanied by a responsible adult. Entry is free, however voluntary donations to our running costs and the maintenance/enhancement of the club's growing games collection are greatly appreciated.

name: Monday Night Games at The Table, Cathays
short_description: Board game group that meets every other Monday evening at The Table cafe in Cathays
full_description: We meet at The Table (Pentyrch Street, Cardiff) every other Monday evening at 7.30pm to play games together. People bring along games to suggest to the group, and we'll often split off into different games depending on how many of us are there, and what games we want to play. It's free to come along and everyone is welcome to join us. All games are explained beforehand, so no need to know anything in advance of coming along. So if you're looking for some midweek gaming, or are just around on a Monday evening and are looking for something to do, then it would be great to have you with us at some point!

name: Eastbourne Board Gamers
location: Function room (upstairs), The Crown & Anchor, 15-16 Marine Parade.,Eastbourne,BN21 3DX
short_description: A friendly weekly club for anyone looking for games in the area.
full_description: Based in Eastbourne, East Sussex we are a growing community looking to connect Tabletop enthusiasts and casual gamers alike. We meet every Monday from 6pm at The Crown & Anchor pub on Marine Parade. We occasionally have longer Sunday sessions and big Charity events as well as raffles. Please feel free to drop by and say hello, bring your own games with you or borrow one from our collection ☺️

name: CLoBaR (Cowdenbeath League of Boardgamers and Roleplayers)
short_description: A twice-weekly community tabletop club serving South Fife.
full_description: We are a tabletop gaming club running from the Maxwell Centre in Cowdenbeath. We lean toward cooperative and narrative-driven games, though we are happy to play anything. The centre is a comfortable venue with plenty of adaptable play area and great facilities. CLoBaR is here for anyone and everyone who wishes to meet round tables and play games. From cards to boards to miniatures, family classics to deep strategy to storytelling and legacy games, we are open to (just about) anything.

name: Crossfire Gaming Club
short_description: Wargaming club based just outside of Glasgow in Cumbernauld that primarily play Warhammer 40k but also do other games
full_description: We meet on Thursdays from 6-10pm We are a friendly and welcoming group that are always open to new players coming along. We are primarily a wargaming club with a lot of Warhammer 40k and other Games Workshop games being played but there are the occasional other games as well as a pretty active Board Game group. If you are new to wargaming, getting back into or just want to try it out we are more than happy to arrange some intro games for the systems you are interested in. The club also owns a few armies and factions of its own for different games so that you don't need to worry about having your own models. First night is free and after that we charge £5 per person per night.

name: Bridport Board Game Meet-up
frequency: We try to meet once a month & usually on the last Saturday when we do from 1pm - 9pm.
short_description: Friendly Board game community meet-up in South West Dorset for experienced gamers or those new to the hobby.
full_description: All are welcome at our tables. When? Last Saturday of each month, 1pm - 9:00pm . Please check our Facebook group page with latest event information. On occasions we do skip a month. So please check first before traveling. Where? Bradpole Forster Memorial Hall, Higher Street, Bridport DT6 3JA . It is £5 per person for the day. We provide a small basic tuck shop so it's always good to have a bit of cash with you. You are also welcome to bring your own food & drinks. Folks tend to order in take aways around dinner time. There is a STRICKLY NO ALCOHOL policy in place. So, if you enjoy the hobby or are looking for a fun new hobby to get into why not get in touch? We are a friendly bunch! Open to all, any background, orientation, or identity. We value and promote diversity within the tabletop gaming community. And whether you're an experienced gamer, or completely new to the hobby, or anywhere in between, there's a place for you at the table. You do not have to be local to attend. Our mission is to grow the community. Happy gaming!

name: Croydon Board Gamers
short_description: Modern tabletop board and card games group based in Croydon, South London
full_description: This group is for anyone interested in modern tabletop board games and card games. We normally play at the Ludoquist board game café in central Croydon, South London. We play games like Codenames, Wingspan, Terraforming MARS, Settlers of Catan, Everdell, Azul, Gaia Project, Eclipse, Clans of Caledonia, Viticulture, Brass, Avalon, Century Spice, Architects of the Western Kingdom, 7 Wonders, Istanbul. All our events are organised via our Meetup group - join our group and RSVP to attend. Please read our Q&A , our Meetup Rules and Code of Conduct for more information.

name: Kirkcaldy Gaming Society
frequency: Monday 18:00 - 21:00 Tuesday 18:00 - 21:00 Wednesday 18:00 - 21:00 Thursday 18:00 - 21:00 Friday CLOSED Saturday 11:00 - 16:00 Sunday 12:00 - 18:00
short_description: we are a non-profit group dedicated to ensuring Kirkcaldy and the surrounding area has access to an inclusive, welcoming and comfortable space where all kinds of games can be played.
full_description: Kirkcaldy had a gaming space well before we formed our society. The community were supported by our Local Friendly Gaming Shop, Kingdom Comics , for several years until the store had to close in early 2023. Each night, the retail space would convert into a small area for groups to play specific games. The store closing meant that lots of small groups of friends all faced the same simultaneous problem; Where are we going to play now?! The solution was deceptively simple. We pooled our resources, formed a committee, and went hunting for a new place to call home. We currently play a wide variety of games, 6 days a week, based in The Hive on Whytescauseway in Kirkcaldy. As Fife’s LGBT+ Centre, their ideals of inclusivity, safety and community are a perfect match for us and we’re proud to support them in their work too. We want to grow, so that all kinds of people can congregate for a common, fun, purpose; Playing Games!

name: Leven Gaming Society
short_description: Tabletop gamers in our local community centre
full_description: Leven Gaming Society or L.G.S is a community driven group providing space for Tabletop, Roleplay, Boardgaming and Card Gaming. We have free demonstration tables for Warhammer 40k and other games so if you want to learn feel free to drop us a message or pop in. Anyone is welcome, we are in the upstairs hall. Under 13's must be accompanied by an adult and remember you don't pay if your only accompanying a child, so feel free to come in and see us. 🤩🤩 Also if it's your first visit then your visit is completely free! otherwise entry is only £3.

name: Cannock Games Club (Sunday)
location: St Lukes Church Hall, Church Street,Cannock,WS11 1DE
short_description: A games club in Cannock, Staffordshire - 25+ years
full_description: The club runs every Sunday evening from 6pm to 10pm at the St Lukes Community Centre in Cannock. Most meetings we have a variety of RPGs such as Dungeons and Dragons, Pathfinder, Cthulhu ...etc, along with the occasional mixture of boardgames and wargames. We opperate on a rolling six week timetable, typically a game will run for a block of six consecutive weeks ( a game block ). Then at the start of week four we gather together and discuss who would like to run what game for the next block. It's a great opportunity for players and Dungeon Masters (the players who run the game!) to decide what they would like to do for the following six weeks. The club operates under a not-for-profit direction of a Committee with a small attendance fee to pay for the room hire and to invest in games

name: October wargames association
location: Mosley all services club 91 Church Road Mosley,Birmingham,B13 9EA
short_description: War games, board games, anything in between
full_description: We are board games and war gamers who enjoy the hobby and meeting new and like minded people.

name: South Birmingham Boardgames & Wargames Club
location: Moseley All Services Club, 91 Church Road,Birmingham,B13 9EA
frequency: Friday evenings 6.30pm - 11pm and Sunday afternoons/evenings 1pm until 11pm
short_description: Welcoming group of gamers, who meet Friday & most Sundays
full_description: Welcoming group of gamers, who meet Friday evenings & most Sundays afternoons. We meet at Moseley All Services Club (social club based on Church Road) - free parking, cheap bar and refreshments (hence group sometimes called Moseley Meeples) Nice large tabletop covers. Wheelchair accessible (does involve deployable ramp so let us know if assistance needed) Currently £1 donations each meeting to cover room hire.

name: Middle-Earth SBG: UK Worcester Community
frequency: We play every Thursday at the Worcester War Games Shop
short_description: Wargaming club at Worcester Wargames playing Middle Earth Strategy Battle Game
full_description: Middle-Earth Strategy Battle Game for players in and around Worcestershire! We have the same policies as the GBHL group page in regards to behaviour and attitude of our amazing community. Use this group to share any of your SBG related questions, works in progress or to arrange times and events to play the game.

name: Hemel Games club
location: Grovehill Community Centre, Henry Wells Square,hemel hempstead,HP2 6BJ
short_description: Hemel Hempstead Games Club
full_description: Hemel Games Club is based at the Grove Hill Community Centre in Hemel Hempstead. We meet every Tuesday from 7:45pm onwards to play board games, role play and run sanctioned Magic tournaments. Most Board Games start at 8pm. We have over 100 members and get about 15 attend each week. It's a great place to make new friends and discover new games. Membership costs £3.00 a week but your first week is free, so why not give it a try? :)

name: Kings Lynn - whatahoot
short_description: Boardgame club for all enthusiasm levels
full_description: We've got a trial night arranged at Whatahoot in Kings Lynn! The first night is Wednesday 17th April at 7pm. Free entry. Bring any games you fancy playing :)

name: Thomas Corbett
frequency: Every Monday and Friday from 7pm and at least l once a month on weekends.
short_description: Based in the county town of Shropshire. We play everything from Fantasy, Sci-Fi, Historical and more!
full_description: Welcome to the page of SWS. Since starting as an after school group at Priory Boys School, Shrewsbury Wargames Society has evolved over the years into a club dedicated to recreating, educating and keeping alive local and world history in all miniature forms. Meeting every Mondayand Friday at 7pm and on at least one weekend a month we play a wide range of both Fantasy and Historical wargames across all periods, including all of Science Fiction settings, alternative histories and even board games. If you want to play it, we do too! With open tables and plenty of friendly faces all you need to bring is yourself to ensure a warm welcome. We are based on the north side of Shrewsbury less than five miles from the site of the battlefield of 1403 with plenty of parking but if you ever need a lift please shout. If you would like to join us or simply want to know more please drop us a message. There is no need for you to have your own figures and we encourage all members from 14 years old upwards. Please also now visit us at our website at http://shrewsburywargamesociety.org.uk/. We look forward to welcoming you to our club, we are sure you will instantly feel right at home. Always welcoming new members.

name: Taunton Board Gamers
short_description: A welcoming, inclusive club, playing a wide range of hobby games
full_description: An established group since 2014, when five board gaming strangers met in a corner of the local tabletop minis club, we meet on Fridays from 1800 until we think it's time to go home and get some sleep. We typically have 20+ gamers in a session, and anybody is welcome to join us that wants to play games, including people with little to no experience of this hobby. The only restriction we have is attendees must be 18+. Club etiquette requires tolerance for all.

name: Crawley Gaming Club
short_description: A weekly hobby club for all types of games.
full_description: A weekly hobby club that caters for all types of games be it war, card, board & role play games. we also play host to the official Horsham & Crawley Pokémon tcg league. We operate every Monday evening from 6pm through to 11pm

name: Birmingham Central Role Players (BCRP)
location: Bournbrook & Selly Oak Social Club, 13a Hubert Road, Selly Oak,Birmingham,B29 6DX
short_description: Birmingham based gaming club for role-players who meet weekly.
full_description: We are a group of dedicated tabletop role-players who meet on Thursdays in Selly Oak. We have members who play everything, from D&D to Traveller, so whether it's fantasy, sci-fi, horror, or cyberpunk, chances are there'll be something to interest you. We meet on Thursday nights from about 6:30ish at Bournebrook and Selly Oak Social Club, and stay until we get kicked out, or the GM has had enough (whichever happens first). But we're not just about roleplaying. We also arrange social events such as pub quizzes (with nerd-related themes, naturally), mix-up games, and a bi-annual social gathering in the city centre. We have a small group of Magic: The Gathering players and some tables occasionally run board games between campaigns. So come along, roll some dice, drink some beer (or non-alcoholic beverages if you prefer), and make some friends! When you arrive, your first night will be free, and this will be your chance to wander around and find out what each table is currently playing, who has space etc. Following that, weekly subs are £3 a night for members and membership for a year is £5 but you get a 10% discount on most purchases at Wayland's Forge (A Roleplaying Games shop in the Custard Factory Birmingham) when you present your card.

name: Southampton Guild of Roleplayers
location: Dice and Dumplings Marlands Shopping Centre,Southampton,SO14 7SJ
short_description: The largest, friendliest, most inclusive club dedicated solely to RPGs in Hampshire!
full_description: As featured on Morrus' Unofficial Tabletop RPG Talk ! Schedule We meet Wednesday's 49 weeks a year! We take the Christmas social, Christmas and New Year off. This means that we're very reliable! We muster at 6.30pm and play from 7 till 10. Structure We run in 3 month seasons and at the last Wednesday of every month we have a “One Shot Wednesday” so people can try new games. This means that stories have endings but you can always keep going if you want and there are regular breaks for GMs and opportunities for new GMs to run without committing to a full 3 months. Games We have a thriving and welcoming community which includes LGBQT and people of colour. We strive for a safe and welcoming environment and as a result have several game designers who regularly attend. We run all sorts of games, from Advanced Fifth Edition to Warhammer Fantasy Roleplay via City of Mists, D&D, Exalted, Mouseguard and Skill Issue! No experience is necessary to play! Just turn up!

name: Chelmsford Games Night
short_description: Tuesdays at the Globe Rainsford Road
full_description: Friendly games night open to all. Find us on meetup.com at Geekzone Chelmsford

name: Breckland Boardgamers
location: The Kings Arms, Middle Street, Watton,Thetford,IP256AG
frequency: Every Monday at 7.30pm at the Kings Arms Pub, 1st and 3rd Thursday of the month at Community Centre
short_description: Tabletop Gaming Club in Watton, Norfolk
full_description: A friendly local tabletop games club in Watton, near Thetford in Norfolk Where do we meet? Every Monday - Kings Arms, Middle Street, Watton IP256AG (what3words location: prettiest.glares.informer) 1st and 3rd Thu of the Month - Watton Youth and Community Centre, Harvey St, Watton, IP256EB (what3words location: camped.gobblers.score) When do we meet? Monday Nights and some Thursdays (see above) (keep an eye on group to ensure the next session hasn’t been cancelled / moved) - Official Start Time 7.30pm - organisers will be there from 7pm so feel free to turn up any time from then. All games and drinks must be finished and packed up by 11pm Cost Mondays (Kings Arms) - Free Entry - the landlady has kindly allowed us to use the space free of charge. Thursdays - £4 per person entry but includes hot drinks and Squash - if you have never been to any of our club sessions then your first Thursday attendance is free Refreshments Mondays - Soft and Alcoholic Drinks and bar snacks available (only refreshments purchased on site can be consumed on site) Thursdays - some refreshments included (see above) - feel free to bring anything else you like (no shop to purchase on site but shops nearby) Parking Mondays - Parking on roadside near the pub is limited. Closest car park with plenty of space is 2 min walk away on Memorial Way Car Park - free (closest postcode IP25 6BS - what3words location overnight.tortoises.sulk) Thursdays - plenty of parking free on site Are children welcome? Under 18’s are welcome as long as accompanied by and supervised by an adult - be aware though that the types of games usually brought to modern board game meets can be too complex for people under the age of around 12 - if you are thinking of bringing someone under the age of 12 please message an admin for advice. Can I bring my own games? Please do, in fact the group kind of relies on at least some of the attendees bringing games as the number of games available to publicly borrow and play will be limited. Whilst the group is likely to focus on more modern board and card games there are no rules on what games you can bring - if you want to play chess / gin / dominos etc and have like-minded other players who want to play that is fine. Please note that no gambling will be allowed. What to expect on the night? Firstly…. Hopefully a fun night! organisers will be on site to welcome people from 7pm. 7.30pm is the official start time at which point we will do a ‘roll call’ (don’t worry, this isn’t people introducing themselves - this is where those who have brought games can let everyone know what they have brought so everyone knows what is potentially on offer). After this everyone forms their own groups on a game they would like to play together. Organisers will be on hand to ensure everyone has got a game. If you are going to be a little late please let us know in this group and we will do our best to reserve you a spot on a game, however if you are later than 8pm this may not be possible. Are there any ‘club rules’? We are all here to have a great time playing games and perhaps even meet new people / form new friendships. There are only really 3 ‘rules’ to consider. Respect each other - we would like this group to be as inclusive as possible - regardless of background / age / disability etc. Any abuse will not be tolerated and please let us know if you feel you have been subject to abuse / discrimination so we can do something about it. Respect each other’s property - please take care with each other’s games when playing them and if you want to play a game someone else has brought but that they are not running please ask first. Respect the venue - please respect this by taking care of their furniture and only consuming refreshments bought on site. Any questions or concerns please don’t hesitate to ask! Happy Gaming!

name: Mid Suffolk Board Gaming
short_description: Board gaming group for those in the Mid-Suffolk region, from Bury St Edmunds to Ipswich, Thetford and Diss.
full_description: We are a board gaming group based at Wyverstone Village Hall, IP14 4SH. We meet on Fridays 2-3 times per month on the Village Halls public bar nights. Our events are free, and are organised via our Facebook Group.

name: Selby Boardgaming
short_description: The best (and only!?!) boardgaming club operating out of Selby, North Yorkshire. All welcome!
full_description: Come and join the best (and only?) boardgaming club in Selby, for an evening of friendly competition and hilarious* banter. New people are more than welcome - it doesn't matter if you've not played any modern boardgames before, there's usually a group with a spare seat and people are happy to teach (well, some more than others haha, but everyone gives it a good go anyways!). All who can respect the cardboard are welcome - please note, people are kind enough to provide the games, please be respectful of the games & each other! The start time is 6pm, but people usually filter in for the first 30 mins or so and play small games, have a chat, then get something bigger on the table. You can bring games to share, but it's not required at all. There is usually ample parking in the Micklegate car park, which is just a short walk past The Griffin into the rear of The George Inn. We set up in the back room, just look for the people with piles of games, we're not subtle :) * Please note, banter may not be hilarious.

name: Fareham and Gosport Board game lounge
frequency: Every third Friday of the month
short_description: Monthly get together of between 40 and 60 friendly gamers playing everything from light party games through to d&d and Warhammer
full_description: I created a Group 'Fareham & Gosport Board Game Lounge' and advertised it in the local community pages. We started building numbers. What a buzz that was to see the group grow from 2 to 20... 40 .... and more. Then we held our first proper meetup in November 2021 just as Covid was easing. The hall holds 60+. Myself & Heather put out tables and chairs for all those we hoped would come.. 8 people came along but it was the best night of gaming I ever had. These 8 strangers, all sharing the same hobby, sat on the same table and chatted, played games and had the best time. Nowadays we regularly get between 40 + 60 'Meeples' turning up from 18 to 70+ in age and having zero experience, to tens of years. We run a couple of newbies tables to introduce these newbies into the hobby. Playing shorter, lighter games that really show the enjoyment that can be had from this hobby. I love seeing the community spirit, friendliness, willingness to help, socialising and make new friends that comes from these meet ups both physically on the night and online in the group.

short_description: Weekly Teen Tabletop Club
full_description: Kindling Club - Teen Tabletop Club Kindling Club aims to provide a weekly gaming session at the Firestorm Games’ South Wales Gaming Centre in Cardiff for those aged 11-18 ( School Years 7-13 ). Firestorm has great facilities for gaming, with lots of space and tabletop ‘terrain’ designed for the games we will be playing - and an awesome Café too! Each week our find: Game Galore: Each week, experience a different tabletop game and RPGs (D&D?). Explore new gaming worlds, whether you crave strategy, storytelling, or just plain fun. Wargaming and Boardgaming Classics: Engage in epic tabletop battles and exhilarating board game showdowns. Miniature Hobby Art Table: Unleash your creativity at our popular art table. Borrow tools and paints to breathe life into your miniatures, or simply enjoy painting and crafting with fellow enthusiasts. All run by our PlayFramers - DBS checked adult Educational Professionals, and PlayLeaders (volunteering adults and ex-members) - who provide games, expertise, structure and a few materials to the sessions to get people started. Once you join as a ‘ Member ’ we charge £9 per session Check out https://www.playframe.co.uk/playframe/kindling-club for more details!

name: Kirton Area Gaming Club
location: Klassic Park, Ings Road,Kirton in Lindsey.,DN21 4BX
short_description: All kinds of board games, card games, tabletop games welcome. You can bring your own games to play, or join in with others, and there’s always a selection of games to choose from.
full_description: All kinds of board games, card games, tabletop games welcome. You can bring your own games to play, or join in with others, and there’s always a selection of games to choose from. First time is free entry so you can try it out, after that each session is £3 per person. Kirton Area Gaming Group (KAGG) meet on Sundays, 6pm-9pm at Klassic Park Kirton in Lindsey (near Scunthorpe). What games do you play? Whatever you bring! You’re welcome to bring any games you like, Chess, a pack of cards, whatever you’d like to play. In general any Board Game, Card Game or Tabletop game is fine. Previous games we’ve played have included: Cluedo, Settlers of Catan, Happy Little Dinosaurs, Cards against Humanity, Warhammer 40k: Kill Team, Marvel Crisis Protocol, Star Wars: Legion, Horus Heresy, Machi Koro, Gretchinz!, Wiz Wars, Fury of Dracula, Dead of Winter, Escape the Dark Castle, HeroQuest and many more I forget! (We’re also trying to get Some RPGs going like Dungeons and Dragons and Pathfinder. If you can DM RPGs contact us!) How often does the group meet? KAGG meets every Sunday between 6pm and 9pm. Any variation or extra time will be posted on the new page here and on the Facebook page. What age is KAGG for? Under 16s need to be accompanied by an adult, but are welcome. As long as players don’t interrupt/distract other gamers. What facilities are at Klassic Park? Klassic have a large hall where we can set up a lot of tables. There’s plenty of free parking, toilets and a kitchen with (free) tea and coffee. We also have a couple of game mats for table top games and a fold out premade table for 28mm games like Deadzone, Infinity, Warhammer, etc.. Are food and drink allowed? What’s a games night without snacks?! Feel free to bring food & drinks along, we also have access to the kitchen at Klassic Park – and there’s (free) tea and coffee plus milk/sugar available.

short_description: The best RPG club in central Birmingham!
full_description: We are a group of roleplayers who get together to play roleplaying games in central Birmingham (that’s UK, not Alabama). We meet every Thursday night, from around 6:30 to 11 in DIgbeth. If you want to get in touch with us then pop on over to the Role Players Guild Birmingham Facebook group . or the Role Players Guild Birmingham website. But don’t feel like you have to – you’re more than welcome to just come on down! You can also say hello to us on Blue Sky . We currently meet in Hennesseys Bar, 30-31 Allison Street, Digbeth, Birmingham, B5 5TJ – which does amazing food and drink, and we are about 10 minutes walk from New Street station. Here’s a map of how to find us . The only cost is £2 room hire per night. Just some of the games which we have been run recently are: D&D Pathfinder Cthulhu Chivalry and Sorcery Vampire Savage Worlds Tunnels & Trolls Stars Without Number Fate Villains & Vigilantes Rolemaster We are always happy to play something new if you want to come along and run your game If you want a venue for your regular game – come on down! – if you want to get into this amazing hobby – come on down! short_description: Small local board games group.
full_description: Whiteley Board Gamers is a fantastic way to meet new people, and participate in the world of tabletop games: a fun offline way to spend time with others and exercise your brain. Based in north Whiteley, at the Barn Farm, we are part of Whiteley Gamers Guild along with our sister group Whiteley TTRPG Group. We meet on the 1st and 3rd Thursday of the month. We are a social and inclusive environment. Everyone is welcome, regardless of experience level! If you’re new to hobby board gaming, at every event we have a Welcome Newcomers Table for complete beginners. Here new members can reserve a space to play light fun games with one of our more experienced members. If you’re a veteran board game geek, you can use the group to pre-arrange a game or, show up on the night and see, organisers are always happy to help and introduce people. Bring your own games or use those in our modest library. So whether you're looking for: strategy, luck-based games, party games or cooperative adventures, come along to one of our monthly events to share in this vibrant and fast growing hobby.

short_description: A games club in Cannock, Staffordshire - 25+ years
full_description: The club runs every Wednesday evening from 7pm to 10pm at the St Lukes Community Centre in Cannock. Most meetings we have a variety of RPGs such as Dungeons and Dragons, Pathfinder, Cthulhu ...etc, along with the occasional mixture of boardgames and wargames. We opperate on a rolling six week timetable, typically a game will run for a block of six consecutive weeks ( a game block ). Then at the start of week four we gather together and discuss who would like to run what game for the next block. It's a great opportunity for players and Dungeon Masters (the players who run the game!) to decide what they would like to do for the following six weeks. The club operates under a not-for-profit direction of a Committee with a small attendance fee to pay for the room hire and to invest in games

name: Wharfedale Table Top Games
location: Salem Hall, Main St, Burley in Wharfedale, Ilkley LS29 7BT,Burley In Wharfedale,LS29 7BT
frequency: 1st Week of the month - 6pm -10:00pm 2nd Week of the month - 7pm -10:30pm 3rd Week of the month - 7pm -10:30pm 4th Week of the month - 6pm -10:00pm
short_description: COLLECT / BUILD / PAINT /PLAY / READ / SOCIALISE
full_description: A group based in Burley In Wharfedale interested in table top games. We want to provide an inclusive and welcoming community for all ages who enjoy tabletop wargaming, roleplaying games, miniature painting and other similar activities. Bring a game and recruit players or find a game you'd like to try. The regular evenings are as follows: 1st Week of the month - 6pm -10:00pm 2nd Week of the month - 7pm -10:30pm 3rd Week of the month - 7pm -10:30pm 4th Week of the month - 6pm -10:00pm at Burley in Wharfedale Salem Hall. Cost is £5 per session includes a soft drink. Pay as you play. No long term commitments. This cost is just to cover the cost of the renting of the venue. We are not for profit and only want to play games and get enthusiastic about our hobby. All profit goes straight into further games, trips and to supplement quieter periods of time.

name: I’m Board Club
short_description: Game club
full_description: I’m Board Club is your board game group 🎲 It’s a friendly, welcoming club where people meet up to play all kinds of board games together – from quick party games to bigger strategy ones. The club meets once a month on the second Thursday at The Fleece Inn, Elland . Each session usually includes: A selection of games to try (like Just One, Secret Identities, So Clover, Herd Mentality, Sushi Go and more). A free raffle where someone wins a board game to take home. A relaxed, social atmosphere – great for newcomers and regular players alike. It’s designed so anyone can join in, whether you’re completely new to modern board games or already a keen player.

name: Troon Tabletop
location: 28th Ayrshire (Troon) Scout Hall, 30 St Meddans Street,Troon,KA10 6JX
short_description: Board & card games group open to like minded people
full_description: Troon Tabletop is providing a space for people to enjoy board games and meet new friends. Mainly playing board games and party games. Open to players of all games though! When: every Sunday from 1.30pm to 4pm. Where: 28th Ayrshire (Troon) Scout Hall, 30 St Meddans Street, KA10 6JX. Parking at the hall is limited. If the car park in front of the hall is full, please consider parking at the train station and walking down the hill if you can. Cost: £5 entry This covers the cost of the venue and includes a tea/coffee and a biscuit. We can accept cash or card! IMPORTANT: the club does not currently have a PVG certificate in place. We ask that any children under the age of 16 are accompanied and supervised by their parent/guardian when attending.

name: Games for a Laugh (Abingdon Board Games)
short_description: Games for a Laugh is a social board games group based in Abingdon Oxfordshire, open to anyone and everyone.
full_description: Games for a Laugh is a social board games group based in Abingdon Oxfordshire, open to anyone and everyone. We've been running weekly games evenings since summer 2014 . We play a wide variety of board games and card games. Weekly board games evenings are held at The King's Head and Bell on Tuesdays at 7:30pm.

name: Ayr Wargames Club
short_description: Weekly club to play Historical, Fantasy & Sci-fi wargames, board & card games.
full_description: We hold weekly Sunday meetings from 12pm until 6pm at Ayr Masonic Lodge, 26 Fullarton St, Ayr. KA7 1UB Club membership is open to everyone although under 16 years olds must be accompanied by an adult. The meetings have a small fee of £5.00 for adults and £2.50 for under 18 year olds, to cover hall hire, tea and coffee. The first meeting for new members is FREE. An accompanying adult who is not playing any games will not have to pay the attendance fee. We play many different rules sets and are always willing to try something new. New players don't have to worry about not having enough figures to try out a game as someone can bring along an extra army. We also have terrain for members to use. There may be the odd Sunday we are not meeting due to other events, so check our social media accounts as we will post any holidays on them. They will also give you an idea of what games people have been playing recently. The club has been meeting regularly since the 1970's and we are now adapting to new social media for publicity. The club has WhatsApp groups to arrange games and chat amongst members. Drop us an email if you want to come along, or a message on the social media accounts. We can arrange a game and sort out table space and someone to play with if needed.

name: Raven Loonies Tabletop gaming club
frequency: First Saturday of the month 7pm to 10pm
short_description: The Raven Loonies are a tabletop gaming club where players gather to enjoy wargames, RPGs, board games, and card games in a fun, friendly atmosphere.
full_description: Raven Loonies Tabletop Gaming Club The Raven Loonies are a friendly and welcoming over 18s tabletop gaming club based at Ravensthorpe Village Hall, Northamptonshire . We meet on the first Saturday of every month (7PM–10PM) to enjoy an exciting mix of games and good company. Our tables regularly feature everything from big wargames like Kings of War , Gaslands , 40k and Blood Bowl to roleplaying games like Dungeons & Dragons . We also have a selection of board games for you to choose from — or you can bring along your own favourites to share . Card games like Uno and even puzzles often make an appearance too! Whether you’re into epic RPG adventures, commanding armies, or just relaxing with a board game, there’s something here for everyone. We pride ourselves on being social, relaxed, and open to new players. If it’s your first time, it’s free to join — after that it’s just £5 per evening . Come along, pick a game, roll some dice, and be part of the growing Raven Loonies community!

name: Card Codex
frequency: We meet every second Sunday of the month; however we plan on increasing this to every Sunday once the club grows in attendance
short_description: Card Codex is a place for people with a passion for TCG and data - we specialise in collection management and community building..
full_description: Like many of you, our journey with Pokémon started years ago, battling our way through the Elite Four on our Game Boys and trading shiny cardboard in the schoolyard. As we grew, so did our collections, and we quickly realised that managing them was becoming a quest in itself. We wanted a single, reliable place to view every card, check real-time market prices, and geek out over the latest news. We looked around, and while there were some good resources, nothing was quite the all-in-one tool we dreamed of. So, we decided to build it ourselves! It was a critical capture, a bold move, but we knew it was our destiny. Card Codex was born!

name: Saltburn TCG Club
short_description: Inclusive, welcoming, casual, weekly TCG club
full_description: Beginners welcome at our inclusive and friendly TCG club. We play Pokémon, Lorcana, Marvel Champions, One Piece, Digimon, Hanafuda, MTG Commander, Yugioh! and even Beyblade. And we’re open to all TCGs and LCGs. We have Pokémon, Lorcana and Yugioh! loan decks if you don’t have your own and will happily teach you how to play. Your first session is free and then only £2/week. Everyone wins a small prize each week with our D20 roll and we have a player of the week trophy too. We have 3 x 30 min random match up rounds from around 6:30pm. We encourage casual play over meta and proxy, retro and out of rotation cards are encouraged. We look forward to welcoming you soon.

name: Tabletop Legends
short_description: Central Bedfordshire Tabletop Games Club
full_description: A creative, welcoming space for storytellers, dreamers, and dice rollers to escape the everyday and share unforgettable adventures. Tabletop Legends is a Roleplaying, Board Game and CCG club in Biggleswade, Bedfordshire.

name: Bicester Board Gamers
frequency: Every Thursday from 7:30 and regular full day sessions on Saturdays
short_description: A group of 100+ board gamers who meet in Bicester
full_description: Hi there - we've set up Bicester Board Gamers to look for more board and card game players in Bicester, Oxford. We play a huge variety of games from old classics and gateway games through to the new hottest releases. Our members enjoy simple party games right through to complex euro games too. Don't know about games? Why not come along and give them a try. We have a massive selection of games, and are always happy to show you something new. We meet weekly, plus occasional on other nights or weekends, around Bicester. We're a friendly bunch - enjoying games with your friends is far more important than who wins! We have grown this group with a focus around community and friendships and our members enjoy meeting up for other occasions as well as just games! Get in touch if you'd like more info on where and when we meet, or have any questions.

name: Nuneaton Model and Games Club
short_description: Friendly gaming community, playing most table top games
full_description: We are open most Saturdays from 10am to 4pm. Membership fee is £10 per year. Entry fees are £2 for members and £3 for non-members. First visit is free!

name: Wadebridge Gaming Group
short_description: Friendly board game group, playing a mixture of board game and war games
full_description: Wadebridge Gaming Group meet every Thursday and enjoy playing a vast range of games. We play euro games such as Catan and Carcassonne as well as having an active war gaming section enjoying games such as Age of Sigmar and Blood Bowl. You can find more information about our group at wadebridgegames.com where you’ll also find links to our Facebook and Discord pages.

name: D6 Den
short_description: A community driven club, warm and welcoming to all no matter what. Newly formed CIC with a great atmosphere.
full_description: We are D6 Den a hub of social activity, brining people together to play their favourite table top games and enjoy hobbying together all completely free of charge. Guests can also make use of our on site cafe and our shop which offers fantastic discounts.

name: Dee Rudiger / James S Baker
location: Corran Esplanade (location of our events, venue not owned by us),Oban,PA34 5AF
short_description: Oban Based BG Club
full_description: Hello UKBGE, My name is James (he/him) and I represent one half of the Oban based Board Game club - RookHart Games. We attended this years (2025) UKBGE and had the most incredible, awesome experience we couldn't have ever even imagined! We are super excited to attend this year too as we have just purchased our tickets for 2026 - super excited to attend the 20th anniversary - huge respect to everyone in the team, past and present for making 20 wonderful events and nurturing such a great community of gamers in the process <3 The reason for this message is to ask if we could possibly be added to the list of game clubs around the UK on your website (so useful to have a handy map for our road trips). Currently, we are only on Facebook due to that being the most widely used platform in the small town of Oban but also due to the fact that we are just a small team of 2 doing monthly events, however we are hopeful to expand as best we can when we can. So you can find us via our FB Page ‘RookHart Games’ but also we do have a website ‘https://playddbg.wixsite.com/rookhartgames’ (I think that's the right link). We've been doing events for just under 2 years (Jan ‘26 will be our anniversary) and alongside our own events we’ve worked a few events with local Oban based community organisations as well, which has been steadily increasing as well :) Anyways, aside from all that, the main thing is that we'd love to be included on the map of game clubs, I realise that we have not yet been exhibitors at UKBGE as of yet and I'm not sure if that's a pre-requisite of being named on the map (if so that's okay, we're working up to it for 2027 hopefully!) but we'd love to be on there all the same if possible. Additionally there are a few other local game groups near us in including another in Oban (Oban Tabletop Gamers), one in a small village in Ford (Ford Village Games Group) and we've even made connections recently with some game groups in the Outer Hebrides too - though I totally realise that they would need to make contact too before being listed which is fair. Well that's all from me, thank you for taking the time to read my message, wishing you and all the UKBGE team well for all the mass planning and work there is to be done for next year, thank you hugely for creating such an incredible event, Kind regards, James S Baker Of the RookHart Team Peace P.s. the best contact method for us is actually through a different email than the one we signed up with, I will change it but the best contact for us is ‘rookhartgames@gmail.com’

name: West Midlands Wizards
short_description: A tabletop and war gaming club in the West Midlands
full_description: Founded in October 2016, West Midlands Wizards is a hobby club based around the Brierley Hill area. Originally set up by a handful of friends after the downsizing of their local hobby store, WMW has gone on to expand in membership and in scope. The goal of the club is to facilitate our members involvement with, and subsequently their enjoyment of, various TableTop hobbies. We currently meet every Friday between the hours of 18:00 - 23:00 at the Oakfield Community Centre (245 Brettell Lane, Brierley Hill, West Midlands, DY5 3LT) Feel free to join our Facebook group https://www.facebook.com/groups/westmidlandswizards where you can share hobby progress, chat and organise games. if you have any questions or feedback, please contact me directly through messenger, or via email at wmwizardscommittee@gmail.com

name: Sheffield Board Games Club
short_description: Friendly group open to the public
full_description: We meet every Tuesday evening at the University Arms 7pm onwards

name: Millennium Library gaming
frequency: Every 2nd and last Saturday of the month 12:30pm - 4:30pm
short_description: Free public gaming in the library
full_description: Free twice a month gaming on the ground floor of the library, near the returns desk. A range of games, and always with options for those new to modern board gaming. Games will always be taught by those bringing them and no prior knowledge is assumed or required.

name: Amelia Reid
frequency: Sunday Mornings, Monday evenings, Thursday evenings, Friday Enenings and other nights in request
short_description: Tabletop games
full_description: We play tabletop games Mainly: Warhammer 40k Board Games Magic The Gathering Skirmish Games DnD

name: Variable Magers
location: Heaton Mersey Conservative Club,Heaton Mersey, Stockport,SK4 3BS
short_description: Tuesday Night Gaming in South Manchester
full_description: We are a group of gamers who meet in South Manchester (UK) on Tuesday nights. We meet every Tuesday evening, starting from 7:30pm and usually play until 11pm. The Conservative Club asks that we pay £2.50 per person a night, or pay an annual membership fee of £20, which also includes a 5% discount on drinks. New players are always welcome (if it is your first time, just ask the bar staff where to go for the board games). We play a variety of board and card games, but over time the focus of the group seems to have drifted towards medium to heavy euro boardgames though other styles and complexities do also hit the table. We currently operate a Games Master rota system for who chooses games on a given night. If you start to come regularly just speak to the members about being added to the rota. Most weeks we have enough people to get anywhere from two to four games going at once so hopefully there is something on offer for everyone, though we do tend to pick a lot of medium to heave euro games.

name: The Wolves of Ravenswood
location: The Horseshoe Inn, Smithy Lane, Croft,Warrington,WA3 7HQ
short_description: Monthly Blood on the Clocktower games in Warrington!
full_description: We’re a friendly, inclusive group of people happy to lie to, deceive and misinform each other for laughs. Starting January 2026, we meet on the first Monday of every month to play Blood on the Clocktower at The Horseshoe Inn in Croft, Warrington. We always make room for newcomers and are expanding our script selection to cater for those with more complex tastes.

name: Hull and East Riding Boardgame Game Group
short_description: A group catering for a variety of game preferences.
full_description: We usually meet on the 2nd Saturday of each month 9.45-5pm and 4th Saturday 1.30-9.30pm at St Martin's Church on the corner of Anlaby Road and North Road HU4 6DD On the in-between Friday evenings we meet in Willerby. Other meetups are shared in our Facebook group: @HullandEastRidingBoardgameGroup

name: Farnborough Board Games
short_description: Board games and social deduction in Farnborough
full_description: 🎲 What We Play Expect everything from light party games to strategic eurogames, social deduction, engine builders, co-ops, and the occasional chaotic wildcard. Members are welcome to bring games along; there’s always a solid selection available and we’re happy to teach. 🤝 The Vibe We’re sociable, inclusive, and here to have a good time. Many of us also organise extra game nights, attend conventions, and generally find excuses to roll dice together.
